subject: [Portal] Remove hardcoded cassandra password
commit_message:
[Portal] Remove hardcoded cassandra password

Make cassandra password generate automatically and distribute it to
components that use DB.

Remove also hardcoded encryption key.

Issue-ID: PORTAL-944
Signed-off-by: SandeepLinux <Sandeep.Shah@att.com>
Change-Id: I6e579a76efacc7a0921fea7c74a7a9e49347ebd8
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Hi Kyzysztof - Thank you so much for your review/feedback. Now I have used secret for ciper encryption keys, and have committed the code. With respect to dynamically creating passwords externally, I would very much appreciate if we can please defer this to the next release. We need to study this further, specifically since I understand Cassandra docker images is currently created separately and uploaded to Nexus, and username/password are already pre-loaded into this image. We will surely address this in the next release, to ensure all security vulnerabilities are properly addressed. Thank you and hope you can merge this commit. Sincerely, Sandeep

              secretKeyRef:
                name: {{ include "common.release" . }}-portal-cass-creds
                key: login
          - name: CASSA_PASSWORD
            valueFrom:
              secretKeyRef:
                name: {{ include "common.release" . }}-portal-cass-creds
                key: password
          - name: CIPHER_ENC_KEY
            valueFrom:
              secretKeyRef:
                name: release-portal-app-cipherEncKey-secret
                key: password
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /config-input
          name: properties-onapportal-scrubbed
        - mountPath: /config
          name: properties-onapportal
